messages.text: <@U012ETLV6NQ> James gave us the go ahead to use the SBR. Interest of full disclosure for the others at the table, this material was obtained through the consulting company connection. I asked if we could make use of it for other development, and got the green light. <@U012ETLV6NQ> Can you please update on the current plan?

Tomorrow I am planning on making Acrylated epoxidized soybean oil and Acrylated epoxidized high oleic soybean oil emulsions at a solids content that can be discussed. I will then formulate the emulsions so of the amount of total solids, SBR is 10wt% and 20wt% respectively so in total that should generate 4 samples. The benzoyl peroxide initator will be blended in with the AESO prior to emulsification. The amine activator will then be blended in with diethyl succinate as a carrier.

A System) Acrylated soybean oils, Benzoyl Peroxide, SBR latex emulsion
B System) Diethyl succinate and amine activator blend

once the user is ready to create the concrete overlay the 2 systems are blended together and sprayed down. over an hour to 2 hours the radical reaction should take place generating the product. we will do some internal studies to make sure we can hit the target numbers Polyset provided to ensure we are within the ballpark for their sought out properties
if this makes sense i will proceed with this. if it doesnt feel free to make changes
I like it…how would you speed up or slow down the reaction, based on weather/temps?
I would say your variables to play with there are the benzoyl peroxide loading as well as the amine catalyst loading
Agreed. The amine is the more gross control, the peroxide is going to be a bit more fine control I suspect.
In the past I’ve used a can (1 gallon) labeled speed up, or slow down…say per tote used…let’s see how the sample work out.

SIP 1233-speed up, and SIP 1233-slow down. I like it. But yeah, that makes sense. At some point Baker we should talk about how we want to do the testing, in particular the mechanical strength/elongation tests.
Yeah. Since it’s an emulsion water has to be dried off and like casted into a shape mold
